rules.title: OpenAI API key
rules.fix: Revoke the key in the OpenAI dashboard and load it from an environment variable / secret store at runtime; never ship it in the client.

rules.title: Anthropic API key
rules.fix: Revoke in the Anthropic console; proxy model calls through an authenticated backend rather than embedding the key.

rules.title: Credentials embedded in a URL (user:pass@host)
rules.fix: Move credentials out of the URL; use a secret store and an Authorization header over TLS.

rules.title: Azure Storage shared-key connection string
rules.fix: Replace with short-lived SAS issued by an authenticated backend; rotate the existing account key.

rules.title: DB connection string with embedded password
Cleartext password / credential
rules.fix: Do not embed or cache credentials in cleartext. Remove the literal password; authenticate with a token or a value fetched at runtime from a secure store (DPAPI / OS credential manager), and stop the app from caching credential-bearing requests/responses to disk.
rules.description: A password is assigned a concrete quoted literal (password="..."), e.g. in a config, a source file, or a cached HTTP request/response in the app-data / browser cache. Cleartext credentials at rest are directly usable by anyone with read access to the file.

rules.title: Stored password hash / protected credential
rules.fix: Confirm what this protects. If it is a stored authentication secret, ensure it uses a strong, salted, slow KDF (bcrypt / scrypt / argon2) -- not MD5 / SHA1 / unsalted -- and is not a shared static credential. Remove embedded credential material from shipped config where possible.
rules.description: A password / passphrase field holds a hashed or otherwise non-plaintext credential value (bcrypt / argon2 / sha-crypt, or an LDAP {SCHEME} hash). Not plaintext, but still credential material at rest: weak schemes (MD5 / SHA1 / unsalted) are crackable offline, and even a strong hash can be replayed if the app compares it directly.

rules.title: PuTTY private key (.ppk) material
rules.fix: Remove embedded SSH private keys; provision per-device keys at runtime from a secure store.

rules.title: RSA/DSA private key in XML (RSAKeyValue with D)
Hardcoded credential in .NET config appSettings
rules.fix: Do not store credentials/API keys in appSettings. Use DPAPI, Windows Credential Manager, or a key vault and inject at runtime; rotate the exposed secret.

rules.title: Hardcoded cryptographic key in .NET config appSettings
rules.fix: Never ship a symmetric or private key in config. Derive a per-user key (DPAPI) or fetch from a key vault; rotate the exposed key. With key+IV+ciphertext all present, the protected value is trivially decryptable.

rules.title: Hardcoded AES/CBC initialization vector in config
rules.fix: Do not hardcode the IV. Generate a fresh random IV per encryption and store or transmit it alongside the ciphertext.

rules.title: Database connection string with embedded password
rules.fix: Use integrated/Windows authentication or a secrets store; never embed the SQL password in a connection string in config or source.

deser_tokens.title: Newtonsoft.Json TypeNameHandling reference
deser_tokens.description: Auto/Objects/All on attacker-reachable input enables RCE via $type gadget chains.

deser_tokens.title: BinaryFormatter reference
deser_tokens.description: BinaryFormatter is unsafe and obsolete (CWE-502). Confirm runtimeconfig EnableUnsafeBinaryFormatterSerialization=false.

callsite_patterns.title: Custom certificate-validation callback present, verify it doesn't return true unconditionally
callsite_patterns.description: Static reference only. Decompile the method to confirm a real check happens.

callsite_patterns.title: Reference to weak hash (MD5/SHA1)
callsite_patterns.description: Acceptable for non-security uses; never for integrity, signatures, or password hashing.

callsite_patterns.title: AES ECB mode reference
System.Random reference (non-cryptographic RNG)
callsite_patterns.description: Use RandomNumberGenerator/RNGCryptoServiceProvider for tokens, IVs, salts, keys.

callsite_patterns.title: Insecure-temp-file pattern reference
Raw ADO.NET command construction (verify SQL is parameterized)
callsite_patterns.description: Presence of a raw command object is not itself a bug. Decompile the call site: if the CommandText is built by string concatenation/interpolation with external input, this is SQL injection. Parameterized queries are safe.

callsite_patterns.title: Process/shell execution API (verify arguments are not attacker-controlled)
callsite_patterns.description: Decompile the call site. If the filename or arguments incorporate external input (config, IPC, URL, file content) without validation, this is command/argument injection. UseShellExecute=true with a user-controlled verb/path is especially dangerous.

callsite_patterns.title: Dynamic XAML / ObjectDataProvider load (RCE gadget if input is attacker-controlled)
callsite_patterns.description: Runtime XAML loading (XamlReader.Load/Parse, XamlServices) and ObjectDataProvider can instantiate arbitrary .NET types and invoke their methods from markup -- the classic XAML/ObjectDataProvider remote-code-execution gadget (e.g. launching a process). Decompile the call site: if the XAML or the ObjectDataProvider target is built from external input (file, config, IPC, network, user paste) without a strict allow-list, this is RCE. Static markup compiled into the app (BAML) is not affected.

callsite_patterns.title: UAC-bypass registry hijack key reference (Fodhelper / sdclt / eventvwr class)
callsite_patterns.description: The binary references a per-user shell-open-command key under ms-settings / mscfile / Folder / exefile. Auto-elevating Windows binaries (fodhelper.exe, sdclt.exe, eventvwr.exe, computerdefaults.exe) read these HKCU keys, so writing them lets a standard user run code at high integrity with no UAC prompt. A legitimate app essentially never writes these keys -- decompile to confirm; if the app creates one, it is a UAC bypass / local privilege escalation.

callsite_patterns.title: COM elevation moniker (verify the target CLSID is not user-writable)
callsite_patterns.description: The app launches an elevated COM object via the Elevation moniker. The moniker itself is a legitimate API, but it is only as safe as the target CLSID's registration: if that CLSID (or its HKCU\Software\Classes shadow) is writable by a standard user, the elevated launch can be hijacked into a UAC bypass. Confirm the CLSID lives in HKLM with admin-only ACLs and is not user-overridable.

callsite_patterns.title: Clipboard access API
callsite_patterns.description: App reads or writes the system clipboard. Reading can sniff credentials/secrets a user copied from another app; writing can plant content. Confirm the clipboard is not logged or transmitted.

callsite_patterns.title: Global input capture / screen capture API (keylogger/screenshot surface)
callsite_patterns.description: Low-level keyboard/mouse hooks, async key state, or full-screen BitBlt/CopyFromScreen indicate global input or screen capture. Legitimate for some tools, but confirm the captured data is not exfiltrated or logged in cleartext.

callsite_patterns.title: Token impersonation API (verify RevertToSelf is always called)
callsite_patterns.description: Impersonation primitives. If the app impersonates a client (e.g. over a named pipe) and fails to RevertToSelf on every path -- or impersonates without validating the caller -- a low-priv client can drive a privileged action (EoP).

callsite_patterns.title: Environment-variable lookup (verify not used for DLL/exe/config paths)
callsite_patterns.description: If an environment variable a standard user controls is used to build a DLL/EXE/config path that the app then loads or executes, a user can redirect it (DLL planting / path hijack). Decompile to see what the value feeds.

callsite_patterns.title: LDAP / directory query API (verify filter is escaped)
callsite_patterns.description: If the LDAP filter or DN is built from external input without escaping, this is LDAP injection. Confirm the filter uses parameterized/escaped values.

callsite_patterns.title: Path building / archive extraction (verify traversal is blocked)
callsite_patterns.description: Path.Combine with external input, or zip extraction without entry-name validation, allows ../ traversal (zip-slip) writing outside the intended directory. Confirm extracted/combined paths are canonicalized and constrained.

callsite_patterns.title: Broken/weak symmetric cipher (DES / 3DES / RC2 / RC4)
callsite_patterns.description: DES (56-bit), 3DES (sweet32), RC2 and RC4 are broken or deprecated. If used to protect anything sensitive, the protection is ineffective. Confirm the cipher and key handling; replace with AES-GCM.

callsite_patterns.title: NoSQL query construction (verify input is not concatenated into the filter)
callsite_patterns.description: If a NoSQL filter / document / $where clause is built from external input without sanitization, this is NoSQL injection. Decompile the call site: confirm typed/parameterized filters are used, not string concatenation or a $where with user input.

callsite_patterns.title: Outbound HTTP request API (verify the target URL is not attacker-controlled)
callsite_patterns.description: Triage: if the request URL or host is built from external input (config, IPC, file, a prior server response) without an allowlist, this is SSRF / open-redirect surface. Decompile the call site to confirm the URL is constant or validated. (Backend-side SSRF is tested dynamically against the API.)

callsite_patterns.title: Base64 encoding (verify it is not used in place of encryption)
callsite_patterns.description: Base64 is encoding, not encryption. If a credential / token / PII field is base64-encoded and then stored or transmitted as if protected, the data is effectively cleartext. Confirm sensitive values are encrypted (DPAPI / AES-GCM), not merely base64-encoded.

callsite_patterns.title: Format-string API (verify the format string is constant, not external input)
callsite_patterns.description: TASVS-CODE-4.6. Format strings must be constant. Decompile the call site: if the FORMAT argument (not just the values) incorporates external input, this is a format-string vulnerability - in C/C++ printf-family it is a memory-corruption / info-leak primitive; in .NET it can throw or leak. Confirm the format literal is fixed.

callsite_patterns.title: Email/SMTP API (verify recipients/subject/headers are sanitized)
callsite_patterns.description: TASVS-NETWORK-4.1. If user input flows into recipients, subject, or headers without stripping CR/LF, an attacker can inject extra headers or recipients (email/header injection). Decompile the call site and confirm inputs are validated and newline-stripped before use.
